data.title: Create structural marker tests for confidence level assignment rules
data.description:
## Testing Mode
RED

## Implementation
Create tests/skills/test-onboarding-confidence-assignment.sh with structural marker tests verifying that SKILL.md documents confidence level assignment rules for all 7 dimensions.

Per Behavioral Testing Standard Rule 5, instruction files (SKILL.md) are tested at their structural boundary — awk/grep checks on section headers and key content markers, not content assertions. This test verifies the SKILL.md describes the assignment rules with the required examples.

File to create: tests/skills/test-onboarding-confidence-assignment.sh

Test functions to add:
- test_assignment_rules_section_present: SKILL.md contains a section or subsection describing confidence level assignment (e.g., grep for 'confidence level' and 'assignment' or 'assign')
- test_node_npm_high_confidence_example: SKILL.md documents that node-npm or a recognized stack output maps to 'high' confidence (grep for 'high' near 'node-npm' or 'recognized stack')
- test_unknown_low_confidence_example: SKILL.md documents that 'unknown' stack output maps to 'low' confidence (grep for 'low' near 'unknown')
- test_all_seven_dimensions_assigned: SKILL.md assigns confidence levels to all 7 dimensions (stack, commands, architecture, infrastructure, ci, design, enforcement) in the assignment rules block

These tests must FAIL (RED) before SKILL.md is modified — confirming the test correctly identifies the absence of the assignment rules.

Update .test-index: add entry mapping plugins/dso/skills/onboarding/SKILL.md to tests/skills/test-onboarding-confidence-assignment.sh with RED marker [test_assignment_rules_section_present].

Test filename fuzzy-match check:
- Source: plugins/dso/skills/onboarding/SKILL.md → normalized: "skillmd"
- Test: tests/skills/test-onboarding-confidence-assignment.sh → normalized: "testonboardingconfidenceassignmentsh"
- "skillmd" is NOT a substring of "testonboardingconfidenceassignmentsh"
- Therefore: .test-index entry is REQUIRED (see acceptance criteria)

Test approach (Given/When/Then):
- Given: plugins/dso/skills/onboarding/SKILL.md exists (before Phase 0 is added)
- When: test_assignment_rules_section_present greps for 'confidence level' assignment markers
- Then: grep returns non-zero (RED) — the markers are not yet present

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tests/skills/test-onboarding-confidence-assignment.sh created and executable
  Verify: test -x tests/skills/test-onboarding-confidence-assignment.sh
- [ ] Test fails RED before SKILL.md changes
  Verify: bash tests/skills/test-onboarding-confidence-assignment.sh; [ $? -ne 0 ]
- [ ] Test passes GREEN after SKILL.md adds Phase 0 assignment rules
  Verify: bash tests/skills/test-onboarding-confidence-assignment.sh
- [ ] .test-index entry added mapping plugins/dso/skills/onboarding/SKILL.md to tests/skills/test-onboarding-confidence-assignment.sh
  Verify: grep -q 'plugins/dso/skills/onboarding/SKILL.md' $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/.test-index
- [ ] .test-index RED marker [test_assignment_rules_section_present] present for SKILL.md
  Verify: grep '\[test_assignment_rules_section_present\]' $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/.test-index
- [ ] chmod +x applied to the new test script
  Verify: test -x tests/skills/test-onboarding-confidence-assignment.sh
